2026-03-26

# ============================================================================
# ICRG: Healthy Control vs. Problem Gambler — Paper Analysis
# Author: Cooper Joseph Sharp
# Date:   2026-03-26
# ============================================================================

library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.3.3
library(car)
## Warning: package 'car' was built under R version 4.3.3
## Loading required package: carData
## 
## Attaching package: 'car'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     recode
library(broom)
## Warning: package 'broom' was built under R version 4.3.3
library(pscl)
## Classes and Methods for R developed in the
## Political Science Computational Laboratory
## Department of Political Science
## Stanford University
## Simon Jackman
## hurdle and zeroinfl functions by Achim Zeileis
library(lmtest)
## Loading required package: zoo
## 
## Attaching package: 'zoo'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     as.Date, as.Date.numeric
library(sandwich)
library(tidycensus)
## Warning: package 'tidycensus' was built under R version 4.3.3
library(pROC)
## Warning: package 'pROC' was built under R version 4.3.3
## Type 'citation("pROC")' for a citation.
## 
## Attaching package: 'pROC'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     cov, smooth, var
library(knitr)

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 1. DATA LOADING & PREPARATION
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

setwd("~/Documents/GitHub/2025-ICRG")
df     <- read.csv("derivatives/scoring/analytic-sample.csv")

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 2. ANALYTIC SAMPLE — HC (0) vs. Problem Gamblers (1)
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

dat <- df %>%
  filter(pg != 1) %>%
  drop_na(
    pg, demo_gender, income_median_split,
    TotalCrime, unemployed, gini, demo_yrs,
    rew_composite,
    dospert_soc, dospert_inv,
    dospert_hs, dospert_ethic, dospert_rec,
    susd_depression, susd_mania, scared_total,
    uclal_total, bisbas_bas, bisbas_bis, sas_total, planfulness_mean
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    PG     = ifelse(pg == 2, 1L, 0L),
    gender = factor(demo_gender),
    income = factor(income_median_split),

    z_age           = as.numeric(scale(demo_yrs)),
    z_yoe           = as.numeric(scale(ses_yoe)),
    z_fevs          = as.numeric(scale(fevs_total)),
    z_usidep        = as.numeric(scale(usidep_total)),

    z_reward        = as.numeric(scale(rew_composite)),
    z_dospert_inv   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_inv)),
    z_dospert_ethic = as.numeric(scale(dospert_ethic)),
    z_dospert_soc   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_soc)),
    z_dospert_hs    = as.numeric(scale(dospert_hs)),
    z_dospert_rec   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_rec)),

    z_depression    = as.numeric(scale(susd_depression)),
    z_mania         = as.numeric(scale(susd_mania)),
    z_anxiety       = as.numeric(scale(scared_total)),
    z_loneliness    = as.numeric(scale(uclal_total)),
    z_planfulness   = as.numeric(scale(planfulness_mean)),

    z_neigh_disadv = rowMeans(cbind(
      as.numeric(scale(TotalCrime)),
      as.numeric(scale(unemployed)),
      as.numeric(scale(gini))
    ), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(gender %in% c(1, 2))

cat("Analytic N =", nrow(dat), "\n")
## Analytic N = 551
table(dat$PG, useNA = "ifany")
## 
##   0   1 
## 378 173
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 3. HIERARCHICAL LOGISTIC REGRESSION
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Block 1: Demographics + neighbourhood context
m1 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe,
  data = dat, family = binomial
)

# Block 2: + Reward sensitivity & risk-taking
m2 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec,
  data = dat, family = binomial
)

# Block 3: + Clinical/social symptoms
m3 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_planfulness,
  data = dat, family = binomial
)

# Block 4: + Financial vulnerability
m4 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_planfulness +
    z_fevs + z_usidep + income,
  data = dat, family = binomial
)


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 4. MODEL COMPARISONS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("\n── Likelihood-ratio tests ──\n")
## 
## ── Likelihood-ratio tests ──
anova(m1, m2, test = "Chisq")
## Analysis of Deviance Table
## 
## Model 1: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe
## Model 2: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec
##   Resid. Df Resid. Dev Df Deviance  Pr(>Chi)    
## 1       543     436.51                          
## 2       537     293.33  6   143.18 < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(m2, m3, test = "Chisq")
## Analysis of Deviance Table
## 
## Model 1: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec
## Model 2: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec + z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness + 
##     z_planfulness
##   Resid. Df Resid. Dev Df Deviance Pr(>Chi)   
## 1       537     293.33                        
## 2       532     276.91  5   16.416 0.005753 **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(m3, m4, test = "Chisq")
## Analysis of Deviance Table
## 
## Model 1: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec + z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness + 
##     z_planfulness
## Model 2: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec + z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness + 
##     z_planfulness + z_fevs + z_usidep + income
##   Resid. Df Resid. Dev Df Deviance Pr(>Chi)    
## 1       532     276.91                         
## 2       529     249.53  3   27.384 4.89e-06 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
data.frame(
  Model       = c("M1: Context", "M2: + Reward/Risk",
                   "M3: + Clinical", "M4: + Financial"),
  AIC         = round(AIC(m1, m2, m3, m4)$AIC, 1),
  BIC         = round(BIC(m1, m2, m3, m4)$BIC, 1),
  McFadden_R2 = round(c(pR2(m1)["McFadden"], pR2(m2)["McFadden"],
                         pR2(m3)["McFadden"], pR2(m4)["McFadden"]), 3)
) %>% kable()
## fitting null model for pseudo-r2
## fitting null model for pseudo-r2
## fitting null model for pseudo-r2
## fitting null model for pseudo-r2
Model AIC BIC McFadden_R2
M1: Context 452.5 487.0 0.363
M2: + Reward/Risk 321.3 381.7 0.572
M3: + Clinical 314.9 396.8 0.596
M4: + Financial 293.5 388.4 0.636 
cat("\n── VIF (M4) ──\n")
## 
## ── VIF (M4) ──
vif(m4)
##                     GVIF Df GVIF^(1/(2*Df))
## z_neigh_disadv  1.118290  1        1.057492
## gender          1.152127  1        1.073372
## z_age           1.388714  1        1.178437
## race            1.403154  3        1.058078
## z_yoe           1.233921  1        1.110820
## z_reward        1.301731  1        1.140934
## z_dospert_hs    3.875850  1        1.968718
## z_dospert_soc   3.639695  1        1.907798
## z_dospert_ethic 5.130467  1        2.265053
## z_dospert_inv   3.149096  1        1.774569
## z_dospert_rec   4.371197  1        2.090741
## z_depression    2.575500  1        1.604836
## z_mania         1.906119  1        1.380623
## z_anxiety       2.544775  1        1.595235
## z_loneliness    2.048102  1        1.431119
## z_planfulness   1.772886  1        1.331498
## z_fevs          1.651443  1        1.285085
## z_usidep        1.302430  1        1.141241
## income          1.293253  1        1.137213
cat("\n── HC3 Robust SEs (M4) ──\n")
## 
## ── HC3 Robust SEs (M4) ──
coeftest(m4, vcov = vcovHC(m4, type = "HC3"))
## 
## z test of coefficients:
## 
##                             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)   
## (Intercept)                -1.587847   0.839368 -1.8917 0.058529 . 
## z_neigh_disadv              0.577695   0.221532  2.6077 0.009115 **
## gender2                    -0.534735   0.368113 -1.4526 0.146324   
## z_age                      -0.671642   0.284211 -2.3632 0.018119 * 
## raceBlack                   1.283296   0.964616  1.3304 0.183397   
## raceOther                  -0.107863   0.999836 -0.1079 0.914090   
## raceWhite                   0.457134   0.867373  0.5270 0.598171   
## z_yoe                      -0.032251   0.190685 -0.1691 0.865694   
## z_reward                    0.637034   0.268957  2.3685 0.017859 * 
## z_dospert_hs               -0.434286   0.387798 -1.1199 0.262766   
## z_dospert_soc               0.341764   0.441298  0.7745 0.438664   
## z_dospert_ethic             0.397296   0.517885  0.7672 0.442992   
## z_dospert_inv               0.662540   0.381481  1.7368 0.082430 . 
## z_dospert_rec               0.116170   0.400078  0.2904 0.771534   
## z_depression                0.186110   0.295460  0.6299 0.528760   
## z_mania                     0.459530   0.281398  1.6330 0.102463   
## z_anxiety                  -0.442105   0.321708 -1.3742 0.169367   
## z_loneliness               -0.810721   0.284899 -2.8456 0.004432 **
## z_planfulness              -0.545698   0.248280 -2.1979 0.027955 * 
## z_fevs                      0.664099   0.248755  2.6697 0.007592 **
## z_usidep                    0.648387   0.236423  2.7425 0.006098 **
## incomeBelow Median (<$40k) -0.950385   0.380829 -2.4956 0.012576 * 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 5. ODDS RATIOS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

col_pg <- "purple4"

get_or_table <- function(model) {
  est <- coef(model)
  ci  <- suppressMessages(confint(model))
  data.frame(
    term    = names(est),
    OR      = exp(est),
    CI_low  = exp(ci[, 1]),
    CI_high = exp(ci[, 2]),
    row.names = NULL
  )
}

or_m4 <- get_or_table(m4)

tidy(m4, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(across(c(estimate, conf.low, conf.high), ~ round(., 3)),
         p.value = round(p.value, 3),
         sig = case_when(
           p.value < .001 ~ "***",
           p.value < .01  ~ "**",
           p.value < .05  ~ "*",
           p.value < .1   ~ ".",
           TRUE           ~ ""
         )) %>%
  rename(OR = estimate, CI_low = conf.low, CI_high = conf.high) %>%
  kable()
term OR std.error statistic p.value CI_low CI_high sig
(Intercept) 0.204 0.6956355 -2.2825850 0.022 0.047 0.755 *
z_neigh_disadv 1.782 0.2252371 2.5648304 0.010 1.159 2.814 *
gender2 0.586 0.3514514 -1.5215038 0.128 0.294 1.171
z_age 0.511 0.2585215 -2.5980126 0.009 0.303 0.839 **
raceBlack 3.609 0.7971088 1.6099379 0.107 0.800 18.871
raceOther 0.898 0.8676549 -0.1243158 0.901 0.168 5.225
raceWhite 1.580 0.7034477 0.6498482 0.516 0.422 6.955
z_yoe 0.968 0.1766764 -0.1825408 0.855 0.683 1.370
z_reward 1.891 0.2305892 2.7626335 0.006 1.212 3.003 **
z_dospert_hs 0.648 0.3821671 -1.1363775 0.256 0.303 1.366
z_dospert_soc 1.407 0.3702160 0.9231480 0.356 0.686 2.946
z_dospert_ethic 1.488 0.4650489 0.8543102 0.393 0.601 3.749
z_dospert_inv 1.940 0.3460731 1.9144511 0.056 0.990 3.868 .
z_dospert_rec 1.123 0.4136805 0.2808214 0.779 0.496 2.527
z_depression 1.205 0.2965616 0.6275604 0.530 0.672 2.163
z_mania 1.583 0.2692294 1.7068362 0.088 0.938 2.706 .
z_anxiety 0.643 0.2828471 -1.5630533 0.118 0.362 1.103
z_loneliness 0.445 0.2461130 -3.2941001 0.001 0.270 0.711 **
z_planfulness 0.579 0.2370716 -2.3018292 0.021 0.361 0.916 *
z_fevs 1.943 0.2228449 2.9800934 0.003 1.265 3.043 **
z_usidep 1.912 0.2207717 2.9369112 0.003 1.255 2.996 **
incomeBelow Median (<$40k) 0.387 0.3713358 -2.5593687 0.010 0.185 0.798 * 
or_m4 %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(term = reorder(term, OR)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(OR, term)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = CI_low, xmax = CI_high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = col_pg) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(x = "Odds Ratio (log scale)", y = NULL,
       title = "Odds Ratios — HC vs. Problem Gamblers (M4)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 6. ROC / AUC
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

col_ng <- "pink3"
col_rg <- "purple1"

roc1 <- roc(dat$PG, predict(m1, type = "response"), quiet = TRUE)
roc2 <- roc(dat$PG, predict(m2, type = "response"), quiet = TRUE)
roc3 <- roc(dat$PG, predict(m3, type = "response"), quiet = TRUE)
roc4 <- roc(dat$PG, predict(m4, type = "response"), quiet = TRUE)

auc_table <- data.frame(
  Model = c("M1: Context", "M2: + Reward/Risk",
            "M3: + Clinical", "M4: + Financial"),
  AUC   = round(c(auc(roc1), auc(roc2), auc(roc3), auc(roc4)), 3)
)
kable(auc_table)
Model AUC
M1: Context 0.882
M2: + Reward/Risk 0.945
M3: + Clinical 0.952
M4: + Financial 0.960 
cat("\n── DeLong Tests ──\n")
## 
## ── DeLong Tests ──
cat("M1 vs M2:\n"); print(roc.test(roc1, roc2, method = "delong"))
## M1 vs M2:
## 
##  DeLong's test for two correlated ROC curves
## 
## data:  roc1 and roc2
## Z = -5.6614, p-value = 1.502e-08
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in AUC is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.08512298 -0.04134123
## sample estimates:
## AUC of roc1 AUC of roc2 
##   0.8821299   0.9453620
cat("M2 vs M3:\n"); print(roc.test(roc2, roc3, method = "delong"))
## M2 vs M3:
## 
##  DeLong's test for two correlated ROC curves
## 
## data:  roc2 and roc3
## Z = -1.9901, p-value = 0.04658
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in AUC is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -1.247472e-02 -9.524546e-05
## sample estimates:
## AUC of roc1 AUC of roc2 
##   0.9453620   0.9516469
cat("M3 vs M4:\n"); print(roc.test(roc3, roc4, method = "delong"))
## M3 vs M4:
## 
##  DeLong's test for two correlated ROC curves
## 
## data:  roc3 and roc4
## Z = -2.546, p-value = 0.0109
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in AUC is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.014776851 -0.001921929
## sample estimates:
## AUC of roc1 AUC of roc2 
##   0.9516469   0.9599963
ggroc(
  list(
    "M1: Context"       = roc1,
    "M2: + Reward/Risk" = roc2,
    "M3: + Clinical"    = roc3,
    "M4: + Financial"   = roc4
  ),
  linewidth = 1
) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 1, slope = 1,
              linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60") +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("M1: Context"       = "grey70",
               "M2: + Reward/Risk" = col_ng,
               "M3: + Clinical"    = col_rg,
               "M4: + Financial"   = col_pg)
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.25, y = 0.30, hjust = 0, size = 3.8,
           label = paste0(
             "AUC M1 = ", auc_table$AUC[1], "\n",
             "AUC M2 = ", auc_table$AUC[2], "\n",
             "AUC M3 = ", auc_table$AUC[3], "\n",
             "AUC M4 = ", auc_table$AUC[4]
           )) +
  labs(x = "Specificity", y = "Sensitivity", colour = "Model",
       title = "ROC Curves: HC vs. Problem Gamblers") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

# ── Classification at Youden's J ────────────────────────────────────────────
best_thresh <- coords(roc4, "best",
                      ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity",
                              "specificity", "ppv", "npv"))
cat("\n── Optimal Cut-Point (Youden's J) ──\n")
## 
## ── Optimal Cut-Point (Youden's J) ──
print(best_thresh)
##           threshold sensitivity specificity       ppv       npv
## threshold 0.2658947   0.9248555   0.8809524 0.7804878 0.9624277
predicted_class <- ifelse(predict(m4, type = "response") > best_thresh$threshold, 1, 0)
cat("\n── Confusion Matrix ──\n")
## 
## ── Confusion Matrix ──
table(Predicted = predicted_class, Actual = dat$PG)
##          Actual
## Predicted   0   1
##         0 333  13
##         1  45 160
cat("Accuracy:", round(mean(predicted_class == dat$PG, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")
## Accuracy: 0.895
